subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Number Garden count every seed, flower, and missing path number up to 20.
lesson.objective: I can count and represent numbers to 20.
lesson.concept_explanation: Count and Represent Numbers to 20 means using a math model, saying what you see, and checking that the answer fits.
lesson.common_misconception: Count each object exactly one time.
lesson.real_world_connection: Help the Number Garden count every seed, flower, and missing path number up to 20.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the model and careful counting to solve count and represent numbers to 20 problems.
lesson.mini_lesson: Use visual objects to reason carefully before choosing or writing an answer.

worked_examples.text: Watch the model first, then match it to the answer for count and represent numbers to 20.
Demo: count, compare, or match each part before answering.

hint_ladder: Say the math words out loud.
Check your answer by doing the action again.

guided_practice.prompt: Count the objects. How many are there?
hints: Look at the model carefully.
Count or compare one step at a time.
Check that your answer matches the question.
guided_practice.explanation: There are 3 objects when each object is counted once.

misconception_bank.one_to_one_counting_error: Count each object exactly one time.
misconception_bank.skipped_number: Do not skip a number in the counting sequence.
misconception_bank.numeral_quantity_mismatch: The numeral must match the quantity counted.
misconception_bank.sequence_gap_error: Use the numbers before and after to find the gap.

story.text: Welcome to Number Garden Quest. The garden needs help counting every seed, flower, and missing path number up to 20. Your goal is to count carefully and show a number with a model. You will learn to touch or look at each object one time, say the count in order, and check that the answer fits. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson is about counting and representing numbers to 20. Important words are count, model, answer, compare, and explain. A visual objects model shows a set of things, and each thing should be counted exactly one time. A common mistake is counting an object twice or skipping one, so move slowly and match one number word to one object. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example. The prompt asks you to count the objects and find how many there are. The model shows three objects, so count one, two, three, and stop when every object has been counted once. Step one, read the question. Step two, count each object in the model one time. Step three, check the answer with math words: there are three objects. The final answer is 3. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo shows how to build a count with objects. Watch each object as it is counted, and connect the last number you say to the total. For example, if you see three garden objects, count one, two, three, and the answer is 3. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the one-to-one counting strategy: count each object once, then match the last number to your answer. The model shows objects you can count, such as a small group of three. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question again. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. It may have more objects or a number that is easier to skip while counting. Use a steady count, point with your eyes or finger, and check that the model and answer match. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question, and use the hint if you need help.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Show that you can count a larger group to 20 without skipping or double-counting. Use the same strategy: count every object one time and make sure the last count is your answer. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work in Number Garden Quest. You practiced counting objects, representing numbers, and checking that your answer fits the model. That careful counting helps you solve number problems up to 20. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This is your growth profile. It can summarize your attempts, correct answers, and accuracy so you know how your counting practice went. Your next recommendation will help you keep building careful number sense. You can replay the mission or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
